Leaderboard v4 new Compare LLMs How it works About Contribute Audit GitHub →
Bradley-Terry Elo 9 agents 56 tasks 283 runs updated 2026-05-09 15:07:01

Deep-Research Leaderboard

A live ranking of open-source deep research agents on a sandboxed Magento + Postmill + Kiwix corpus. Each agent runs against the same task set and produces a markdown report. Scores are derived from URL grounding, judge pass rate, and report quality. The reachability gate kills any agent that fabricates URLs, no matter how fluent the prose.

How is the ranking produced? → Add your DR / reproduce the run → Compare across LLMs → GitHub →
Pairs scored
344
across 9 agents x 56 tasks
Unique URLs cited
2,568
distinct sandbox pages across all reports
LLM tokens (est)
1.4M
15,580 judge calls + report generation
Filtered as degenerate
61
runner placeholders / crash stubs dropped from Elo
Backbone LLM DeepSeek V4-flash 283 runs DeepSeek V4-pro queued GPT-4o-mini queued Claude Sonnet 4.6 queued Gemini 2.5 Pro queued

Each agent's composite_v2 is backbone-dependent. The leaderboard below is built on DeepSeek V4-flash. Once the other backbones land, the picker activates and the agent x LLM matrix appears on /compare.

Composite_v2 ranking · multiplicative reachability gate
95% confidence intervals from 1000 bootstrap iterations · click row for drill-down

Coverage is how many of the 57 tasks this agent has a valid score for. Agents with fewer than 30 pairs are marked partial - their Elo will tighten as more pairs land. The full bulk is still in flight for newly-added agents. A small "~" marker on the rank means the gap to the row above is not statistically significant.
674
Bar spans the global Elo range: 95% CI point estimate
1567
Adjacent-rank significance · permutation N=1000
camel-ai › flowsearcher-ds Δ79 p=0.305
flowsearcher-ds › smolagents Δ94 p=0.447
smolagents › ldr Δ362 p=0.0
ldr › gpt-researcher Δ79 p=0.326
gpt-researcher › deerflow Δ44 p=0.573
deerflow › ii-researcher Δ90 p=0.31
ii-researcher › langchain-odr Δ3 p=0.97
langchain-odr › storm Δ11 p=0.896

What you're seeing. Each row is one open-source DR framework. Higher Elo means it more often wrote a more correct, better-grounded report than its peers across the 56 tasks. Read the full method → Click any agent row to see per-pillar bars, best/worst tasks, and the upstream project link.

Bradley-Terry Elo over composite_v2 battles | composite_v2 = reachability x (0.40 url + 0.40 judge + 0.20 spec) | Sandbox: Magento + Postmill + Kiwix | github.com/yibol9768-alt/deep-research-arena
